skilled.metaTitle: UK Skilled Worker & work routes | GOV.UK alignment | MinRosh
skilled.metaDescription: Skilled work routes for the United Kingdom: sponsored employment and visa planning context. Confirm on GOV.UK.
skilled.headline: United Kingdom skilled work routes
skilled.intro: The Skilled Worker route usually requires a licensed sponsor, a job at the appropriate skill and salary level, and English ability. Other work visas exist for specific circumstances. Always read the relevant GOV.UK visa page for your situation; MinRosh helps with sequencing and document thinking before you engage employers or lawyers in the UK.

sections.title: Sponsorship reality
sections.body: Most applicants need a Certificate of Sponsorship from a licensed employer. We help you understand what to clarify with a potential sponsor and what you must prepare on your side.
bullets: Role title versus SOC code fit
English and tuberculosis testing where required

faq.question: Is a UK job offer always required?
faq.answer: For Skilled Worker, a qualifying offer and sponsorship are normally required. Other categories have different rules—check GOV.UK.

partner.metaDescription: Partner and family visa context for the UK. Financial requirement and relationship evidence on GOV.UK.
partner.headline: United Kingdom partner and family visas
partner.intro: Partner and family categories on GOV.UK set out financial thresholds, relationship evidence, and sometimes English requirements. Rules change with immigration statements; verify the current Immigration Rules and guidance.

sections.title: Evidence and planning
sections.body: We help you map relationship history and finances to the categories you are reading on GOV.UK, so you know what to collect before professional review.

student.metaTitle: UK Student visa planning | study in Britain | MinRosh
student.metaDescription: Student route guidance for the UK: CAS, funds, and genuine student factors. Official detail on GOV.UK.
student.headline: Study in the United Kingdom
student.intro: The Student route requires an offer from a licensed student sponsor and a Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies. You must meet financial and English requirements and show a genuine intention to study. Read GOV.UK for the latest maintenance figures and document list.

sections.title: Course and provider
sections.body: We help you sanity-check how your chosen course and location align with your study story and budget.

education.metaTitle: UK education consultation | CAS & visa alignment | MinRosh
education.metaDescription: Institution and course planning for the UK with Student route awareness.
education.headline: Education planning for the United Kingdom
education.intro: Selecting a programme affects your CAS, fees, and living cost story. MinRosh helps align education choices with what UKVI expects to see in a coherent application.

sections.title: From offer to visa prep
sections.body: Once you have a credible CAS pathway, we tie timelines to funds evidence and any dependants.

updates.metaTitle: UK immigration updates | Home Office changes | MinRosh
updates.metaDescription: Tracking UK visa changes via GOV.UK and statements. MinRosh helps clients interpret impact.
updates.headline: United Kingdom immigration updates
updates.intro: UK immigration rules are published in law and guidance and can change with policy statements. Rely on GOV.UK and the Immigration Rules for authoritative text.

sections.title: What to watch
sections.body: Salary lists, financial thresholds, and student maintenance figures are common update points. Note effective dates carefully.

faq.metaDescription: Introductory UK visa questions for clients working with MinRosh.
faq.headline: United Kingdom visa questions
faq.intro: General guidance only. Confirm every requirement on GOV.UK before applying.

faq.question: Do I need ATAS for my course?
faq.answer: Some science and technology courses require ATAS clearance. Your institution should confirm if it applies.

faq.question: Where is the immigration health surcharge explained?
faq.answer: GOV.UK publishes current IHS rates and who must pay as part of most longer UK visa applications.

about.metaTitle: MinRosh & United Kingdom pathways | migration guidance
about.metaDescription: How MinRosh supports UK visa and education planning.
about.headline: MinRosh and United Kingdom visas
about.intro: We help clients who compare Australia with the UK—or who are UK-bound from the region—with structured planning and emphasis on GOV.UK accuracy.

sections.title: Scope
sections.body: We are not a UK law firm; we provide migration-agent-style clarity and triage, and we point to solicitors where UK legal advice is required.

contact.metaTitle: Contact MinRosh | United Kingdom visa enquiry
contact.metaDescription: Contact MinRosh Migration about UK study, work, or family visa planning.
contact.headline: Contact us about the United Kingdom
contact.intro: Tell us your goal—Student, Skilled Worker, partner, or visitor—and your timeline. We will suggest reading on GOV.UK and whether a consultation is the right next step.

sections.title: Connect
sections.body: Use the site contact form or book a consultation; note United Kingdom in your message.
